name: Humpback Whale Pod
description: Cetacean social group demonstrating long-range acoustic communication, cultural song transmission across ocean basins, and cooperative bubble-net feeding. Song patterns evolve year by year and propagate westward across the Pacific — a form of culture operating at oceanic scale.

scope.definition: Proportion of song phrase matches between pods separated by 5,000km, measured annually. Songs change each season but maintain coherence across the population simultaneously.

evidence.source: Noad et al. (2000) Pacific Ocean song transmission study; Garland et al. (2011) cultural evolution of song; 20+ years of hydrophone data.

condition.environment: open ocean, breeding season, males singing

scope.falsifiability: If pods in isolation (island populations) show divergent songs that do not resync when contact resumes, oceanic cultural transmission fails as model.
scope.measurement_limits: Coherence measured during breeding season. Feeding season vocalizations show lower cross-pod matching.

scope.definition: Success rate of cooperative bubble-net feeding maneuvers requiring 3-15 whales to coordinate spiral ascent, exhalation timing, and surface lunge simultaneously.

evidence.source: Wiley et al. (2011) Gulf of Maine bubble-net studies; multi-year behavioral observation.

condition.environment: prey school (herring, krill) in shallow water, group of experienced individuals
constraints: requires learned coordination — naive whales cannot initiate

scope.falsifiability: Novel group of inexperienced whales fails to coordinate net; technique is culturally transmitted, not instinctive.
scope.measurement_limits: Observed primarily in North Atlantic feeding grounds.

attributes.long_range_communication: Song carries 1,000km+ through the deep sound channel (SOFAR). No other land-derived mammal communicates at this spatial scale.
attributes.cultural_transmission: Song evolves each year via social learning. New phrases originate in one region and spread across the ocean basin within a single season — demonstrating rapid, basin-wide cultural updating.
attributes.migration_range: 20,000km round trip annually between tropical breeding grounds and polar feeding grounds.
attributes.pattern: acoustic culture, basin-scale social coherence, intergenerational learning, seasonal cycle fidelity

links.note: Both encode memory in acoustic/temporal patterns that persist across geography.
Both demonstrate population-level coordination without central command, at vastly different scales.
Bubble-net feeding traces a logarithmic spiral — the same geometry as galaxies and shells.
Humpback song encodes seasonal and generational time in acoustic structure.
